# codex-swarm v0.7.3

This release gives the machine-global SQLite coordination ledger an accurate
default filename.

## Fixed

- New installations create `state.db` instead of a SQLite database named
  `state.json`.
- Existing installations continue using a legacy-only `state.json` ledger
  until it is migrated with the daemon stopped.
- If both files exist, `state.db` is selected deterministically.
- The CLI, daemon, and Windows service share the same state-path resolver.

## Upgrade safety

- Default-path resolution never renames, copies, or deletes state.
- Explicit `--state` and `CODEX_SWARM_STATE` paths remain unchanged.
- The existing JSON-content-to-SQLite migration and its `.legacy.json` backup
  behavior are unchanged.

## Verified

- Formatting, vet, the full Go test suite, both production builds, and
  `govulncheck ./...` pass.
- Windows service tests cover the new `state.db` default.
- Resolver tests cover fresh, legacy-only, and both-files installations.
